texts.label: text
texts.orig: The ERG (Emergency Response Guide) provided by Kia describes emergency response operations, warnings, and precautions related to the vehicle. This publication is intended to provide necessary information for vehicle accident rescue operations and for the training and further education of first and second responders.
texts.text: The ERG (Emergency Response Guide) provided by Kia describes emergency response operations, warnings, and precautions related to the vehicle. This publication is intended to provide necessary information for vehicle accident rescue operations and for the training and further education of first and second responders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Please note that the guide may be updated by Kia on an ongoing basis. It is not intended for retailers, end consumers, or any other readers not mentioned in the preceding sentence.
texts.text: Please note that the guide may be updated by Kia on an ongoing basis. It is not intended for retailers, end consumers, or any other readers not mentioned in the preceding sentence.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The provided guide applies only to the Kia EV9 vehicle and includes information about the location and description of high-voltage components and the vehicle's structure. However, it does not cover every scenario in emergency situations.
texts.text: The provided guide applies only to the Kia EV9 vehicle and includes information about the location and description of high-voltage components and the vehicle's structure. However, it does not cover every scenario in emergency situations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Failure to follow the recommended procedures during emergency response may result in death or other serious injuries. It is important to read the guide in advance as it contains necessary information about the vehicle's features and other provided content in the event of an accident.
texts.text: Failure to follow the recommended procedures during emergency response may result in death or other serious injuries. It is important to read the guide in advance as it contains necessary information about the vehicle's features and other provided content in the event of an accident.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A WARNING indicates a situation in which harm, serious bodily injury or death could result if the warning is ignored.
texts.text: A WARNING indicates a situation in which harm, serious bodily injury or death could result if the warning is ignored.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Initial Response: Identify, Immobilize and Disable
texts.text: Initial Response: Identify, Immobilize and Disable

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following procedures should be used whenever you are dealing with a EV9 at an emergency scene. However, all operations should be consistent with your department's standard operating procedures, guidelines, and any applicable laws. When an EV9 is damaged in a crash, the high-voltage safety systems may have been compromised and present a potential high-voltage electrical shock hazard. Exercise caution and wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) safety gear, including high-voltage safety gloves and boots. Remove all metallic jewelry, including watches and rings.
texts.text: The following procedures should be used whenever you are dealing with a EV9 at an emergency scene. However, all operations should be consistent with your department's standard operating procedures, guidelines, and any applicable laws. When an EV9 is damaged in a crash, the high-voltage safety systems may have been compromised and present a potential high-voltage electrical shock hazard. Exercise caution and wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) safety gear, including high-voltage safety gloves and boots. Remove all metallic jewelry, including watches and rings.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The EV9 is an electric vehicle. Emergency responders should respond to emergency scenarios involving the EV9 accordingly, exercising extreme care and caution to avoid contact with the high-voltage system within the vehicle.
texts.text: The EV9 is an electric vehicle. Emergency responders should respond to emergency scenarios involving the EV9 accordingly, exercising extreme care and caution to avoid contact with the high-voltage system within the vehicle.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The EV9 can be easily identified by the car name emblem attached on the tailgate on the left side.
texts.text: The EV9 can be easily identified by the car name emblem attached on the tailgate on the left side.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The car name emblem may be missing or hidden after a crash due to damage to the vehicle. Always be sure to use other identification methods in this section before determining that the vehicle is not an electric car.
texts.text: The car name emblem may be missing or hidden after a crash due to damage to the vehicle. Always be sure to use other identification methods in this section before determining that the vehicle is not an electric car.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The charging port is located on the rear left side.
texts.text: The charging port is located on the rear left side.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note that the doors must be unlocked to open the charge port door.
texts.text: Note that the doors must be unlocked to open the charge port door.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: How to open the charging port
texts.text: How to open the charging port

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Press the unlock door button, depress the brake pedal and apply the Electric Parking Brake (EPB).
texts.text: Press the unlock door button, depress the brake pedal and apply the Electric Parking Brake (EPB).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Turn OFF all switches, move the shift lever to P (Park) and turn off the vehicle.
texts.text: Turn OFF all switches, move the shift lever to P (Park) and turn off the vehicle.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Open the charging door by pressing it, or press the 'charging door open button' in the crash pad.
texts.text: Open the charging door by pressing it, or press the 'charging door open button' in the crash pad.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The vehicle certification label attached on the top of the dashboard through the front windshield gives the VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) as shown in the below picture.
texts.text: The vehicle certification label attached on the top of the dashboard through the front windshield gives the VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) as shown in the below picture.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: The VIN can be also found under the driver's seat (or passenger's seat)
texts.text: The VIN can be also found under the driver's seat (or passenger's seat)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The battery charge status indicator (4 separate lights), located inside the charging door, is illuminated when the vehicle is charged. The number of lights illuminated indicates the charge level of the battery.
texts.text: The battery charge status indicator (4 separate lights), located inside the charging door, is illuminated when the vehicle is charged. The number of lights illuminated indicates the charge level of the battery.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The EV9 Cluster Instrument Panel displays EV specific features such as high-voltage battery SOC (State of Charge) as below.
texts.text: The EV9 Cluster Instrument Panel displays EV specific features such as high-voltage battery SOC (State of Charge) as below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The next step is to immobilize the vehicle to prevent any accidental movement that crash, the vehicle may appear to be shut "READY" mode light is illuminated on the
texts.text: The next step is to immobilize the vehicle to prevent any accidental movement that crash, the vehicle may appear to be shut "READY" mode light is illuminated on the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: can endanger response personnel or civilians. When the EV9 is damaged in a off, even when it actually is not, due to a lack of virtual engine sounds. When the Instrument Panel, the vehicle can move silently using the electric motor.
texts.text: can endanger response personnel or civilians. When the EV9 is damaged in a off, even when it actually is not, due to a lack of virtual engine sounds. When the Instrument Panel, the vehicle can move silently using the electric motor.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Responders should approach the vehicle from the sides and stay away from the front or rear as they are potential paths for vehicle movement. Be sure to immobilize the vehicle in the following manner.
texts.text: Responders should approach the vehicle from the sides and stay away from the front or rear as they are potential paths for vehicle movement. Be sure to immobilize the vehicle in the following manner.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Put the vehicle in P (Park) position by pressing the 'P' button.
texts.text: Put the vehicle in P (Park) position by pressing the 'P' button.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · When installing a block or jack, avoid high-voltage cable, high-voltage battery.
texts.text: When installing a block or jack, avoid high-voltage cable, high-voltage battery.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · If high-voltage components or cables are exposed, do not place any support on them.
texts.text: If high-voltage components or cables are exposed, do not place any support on them.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use standard stabilization (lift) points, as shown beside. Always be sure to connect to a structural component of the vehicle and avoid placing cribbing under high-voltage cables, and other areas not normally considered acceptable.
texts.text: Use standard stabilization (lift) points, as shown beside. Always be sure to connect to a structural component of the vehicle and avoid placing cribbing under high-voltage cables, and other areas not normally considered acceptable.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The final step in the initial response process, conducted after immobilizing the vehicle, is to disable the vehicle, its SRS components and the high-voltage electrical system. To prevent current flow through the system, use one of the following procedures to disable the vehicle.
texts.text: The final step in the initial response process, conducted after immobilizing the vehicle, is to disable the vehicle, its SRS components and the high-voltage electrical system. To prevent current flow through the system, use one of the following procedures to disable the vehicle.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: *In case of emergency SRS : Supplemental Restraint System
texts.text: *In case of emergency SRS : Supplemental Restraint System

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.1 Disabling the System - Smart Key System and 'EV' START/STOP Button
texts.text: 3.1 Disabling the System - Smart Key System and 'EV' START/STOP Button

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Confirm the status of the READY light on the instrument panel.  If the READY light is illuminated, the vehicle is ON.
texts.text: Confirm the status of the READY light on the instrument panel.  If the READY light is illuminated, the vehicle is ON.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) If the READY light is NOT illuminated, the vehicle is off.  Do not push the 'EV' START/STOP button because the vehicle may start (go into READY mode).
texts.text: If the READY light is NOT illuminated, the vehicle is off.  Do not push the 'EV' START/STOP button because the vehicle may start (go into READY mode).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) To turn OFF the system, press the 'P' (Park) button on the rotary shifter dial, and press the EV button.
texts.text: To turn OFF the system, press the 'P' (Park) button on the rotary shifter dial, and press the EV button.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. If necessary, lower the windows, unlock the doors and open the trunk as required, before disconnecting the 12 V battery.  Once the 12 V battery is disconnected, power controls will not operate.
texts.text: If necessary, lower the windows, unlock the doors and open the trunk as required, before disconnecting the 12 V battery.  Once the 12 V battery is disconnected, power controls will not operate.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Before disconnecting the 12 V battery, remove the smart key at least 2 meters (7 feet) away from the vehicle to prevent accidental restart.
texts.text: Before disconnecting the 12 V battery, remove the smart key at least 2 meters (7 feet) away from the vehicle to prevent accidental restart.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Open the hood after pressing the release button twice.
texts.text: Open the hood after pressing the release button twice.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: * If the release button does not work, pull the emergency lever twice after removing the emergency open cover located on the left hand side of the footrest.
texts.text: If the release button does not work, pull the emergency lever twice after removing the emergency open cover located on the left hand side of the footrest.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Separate the 12 V auxiliary battery negative terminal (A) after loosening the nut.
texts.text: Separate the 12 V auxiliary battery negative terminal (A) after loosening the nut.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Remove the PE room junction box cover.
texts.text: Remove the PE room junction box cover.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. Disconnect the high voltage service interlock connector (A).
texts.text: Disconnect the high voltage service interlock connector (A).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Wait more than 5 minutes for capacitor of the high-voltage battery system to be discharged completely.
texts.text: Wait more than 5 minutes for capacitor of the high-voltage battery system to be discharged completely.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2 Disabling the system - IG (Ignition) Fuse Removal
texts.text: 3.2 Disabling the system - IG (Ignition) Fuse Removal

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Remove the PE (Motor) room fuse box cover.
texts.text: Remove the PE (Motor) room fuse box cover.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. If necessary, lower the windows, unlock the doors and open the tailgate as required, before disconnecting the 12 V battery (located in the left side of PE(Motor) room). Once the 12 V battery is disconnected, power controls will not operate.
texts.text: If necessary, lower the windows, unlock the doors and open the tailgate as required, before disconnecting the 12 V battery (located in the left side of PE(Motor) room). Once the 12 V battery is disconnected, power controls will not operate.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. In the event the vehicle is unable to be disabled using the 'EV' START/STOP Button, using the fuse puller located in the PE(Motor) room fuse box, pull both the IG1, IG2 fuse from the PE room fuse box. If the IG fuses cannot be located, pull out all of the fuses and relays in the fuse box.
texts.text: In the event the vehicle is unable to be disabled using the 'EV' START/STOP Button, using the fuse puller located in the PE(Motor) room fuse box, pull both the IG1, IG2 fuse from the PE room fuse box. If the IG fuses cannot be located, pull out all of the fuses and relays in the fuse box.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Remove the service interlock connector and disable the high-voltage battery.
texts.text: Remove the service interlock connector and disable the high-voltage battery.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · If previously mentioned methods of disabling the vehicle's system are unsuccessful, any emergency procedures involving the electric vehicle may cause the accidental deployment of undeployed airbags and electric shock from high-voltage components.
texts.text: If previously mentioned methods of disabling the vehicle's system are unsuccessful, any emergency procedures involving the electric vehicle may cause the accidental deployment of undeployed airbags and electric shock from high-voltage components.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Before engaging in any emergency response procedures, ensure the vehicle is disabled and wait 5 minutes to allow the capacitor in the high-voltage system to discharge to avoid electrocution.
texts.text: Before engaging in any emergency response procedures, ensure the vehicle is disabled and wait 5 minutes to allow the capacitor in the high-voltage system to discharge to avoid electrocution.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Exposed cables or wires may be visible inside or outside the vehicle. Never touch the metal chassis wires, cables, connectors, or any electric components before disabling the system.
texts.text: Exposed cables or wires may be visible inside or outside the vehicle. Never touch the metal chassis wires, cables, connectors, or any electric components before disabling the system.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Failure to follow these instructions will lead to serious bodily injury or death by electrocution.
texts.text: Failure to follow these instructions will lead to serious bodily injury or death by electrocution.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The EV9 is an electric vehicle. Because of the high-voltage components contained within, first responders should pay special attention when they extract occupants in the car. Before performing any extraction operations, the first responders should 'Identify, Immobilize and Disable' the vehicle as discussed in sections on emergency procedures.
texts.text: The EV9 is an electric vehicle. Because of the high-voltage components contained within, first responders should pay special attention when they extract occupants in the car. Before performing any extraction operations, the first responders should 'Identify, Immobilize and Disable' the vehicle as discussed in sections on emergency procedures.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When responding to an incident involving the EV9, we recommend that the first responders follow their organization's standard operating procedures for dealing with vehicle emergencies. When the first responders cut the vehicle, they should always pay special attention to the airbag system, orange colored high-voltage cables and other high-voltage components so that the parts are not damaged and to prevent a risk of explosion or electrocution.
texts.text: When responding to an incident involving the EV9, we recommend that the first responders follow their organization's standard operating procedures for dealing with vehicle emergencies. When the first responders cut the vehicle, they should always pay special attention to the airbag system, orange colored high-voltage cables and other high-voltage components so that the parts are not damaged and to prevent a risk of explosion or electrocution.

texts.orig: 4.3 Door open When the door outer handle doesn't pop-up, push the front side inwards.
texts.text: 4.3 Door open When the door outer handle doesn't pop-up, push the front side inwards.

texts.orig: When the door outer handle doesn't pop-up, push the front side inwards.
texts.text: When the door outer handle doesn't pop-up, push the front side inwards.
